name: Teletext
classification_notes: Humanist pixel typeface originally designed for airport display screens. Bitmap-influenced construction with rounded pixel grid.
summary: A humanist pixel typeface originally drawn by Adrian Frutiger in 1979 for Paris CDG airport screens, revived and released by Maxitype in 2025.
description: Teletext was designed by Adrian Frutiger in 1979 for the new Charles de Gaulle Airport departure boards, and remained unpublished for decades. Maxitype's 2025 release brings it to market with four weights, preserving its characteristic pixel-grid construction and humanist proportions. It occupies a rare position as a signed pixel type by one of the twentieth century's foremost type designers.
foundry_blurb: A humanist pixel typeface designed by Adrian Frutiger in 1979 for Paris CDG airport screens, released by Maxitype in 2025.

name: Maligne
classification_notes: Expressive display typeface with 16 styles across a broad stylistic range. Designed collaboratively by Maximage and Isia Yurovsky.
summary: A bold collaborative display typeface by Maximage and Isia Yurovsky available in 16 styles.
description: Maligne is a display typeface developed collaboratively by Swiss graphic design studio Maximage and designer Isia Yurovsky. It spans 16 styles and is sold alongside a companion booklet documenting the design process. The name and aesthetic reflect a wilful expressive energy typical of Maxitype's research-driven approach.
foundry_blurb: Maligne is a typeface by Maximage and Isia Yurovsky, available in 16 styles, accompanied by a publication documenting its development.

name: Rhymes
classification_notes: A large serif family by Prague-based designer Jakub Samek, released 2022. 32 styles spanning a wide range of weights and variants.
summary: A 32-style contemporary serif family by Prague-based type designer Jakub Samek, released through Maxitype in 2022.
description: Rhymes is a substantial serif type family designed by Jakub Samek, a Prague-based graphic and type designer. Released in 2022, it spans 32 styles and is Maxitype's largest family offering. The typeface reflects a careful study of serif conventions filtered through a contemporary Central European design sensibility.
foundry_blurb: Rhymes is a contemporary serif family by Jakub Samek, comprising 32 styles across a full weight range.

name: Signs
classification_notes: Display sans-serif by Maximage with 16 styles spanning Thin through Black. Used in a published type specimen featuring Nike branding typography as specimen text.
summary: A bold geometric sans-serif by Maximage spanning eight weights, distributed with a limited-edition type specimen.
description: Signs is a geometric sans-serif typeface designed by Maximage with 16 styles from Thin to Black. It was published alongside a limited-edition type specimen (350 copies) designed with Marietta Eugster and Åbäke. The specimen uses Nike brand nomenclature as typographic raw material, positioning Signs firmly in the territory of high-energy graphic branding.
foundry_blurb: Signs is a typeface by Maximage, accompanied by a limited-edition softcover type specimen designed in collaboration with Marietta Eugster and Åbäke.

name: Selecta
classification_notes: Grotesque typeface presented through a type specimen comparing it to historical grotesque models. 12 styles including variable. Research-focused design informed by historical type specimen analysis.
summary: A research-driven grotesque by Maximage presented through a type specimen charting its relationship to historical models, available with a variable axis.
description: Selecta is a grotesque sans-serif designed by Maximage and documented in a 32-page type specimen by Giliane Cachin that maps the design against historical grotesque precedents. Released in 2022, it offers 12 styles including a variable font. The project exemplifies Maxitype's approach of treating type releases as research publications.
foundry_blurb: Selecta is a grotesque typeface by Maximage, presented through a type specimen by Giliane Cachin that places it in dialogue with historical grotesque typefaces.

name: Mosaic
classification_notes: Two-style display typeface by Zürich-based designer Clemens Piontek, released 2024. Includes Regular and Outline variants.
summary: A modular display typeface by Zürich-based Clemens Piontek with Regular and Outline variants, released in 2024.
description: Mosaic is a display typeface designed by Clemens Piontek in 2024. It comes in two styles — Regular and Outline — and reflects the modular, tile-like construction implied by its name. Piontek is a Zürich-based designer, and Mosaic is his first release through Maxitype.
foundry_blurb: Mosaic is a typeface by Clemens Piontek, available in Regular and Outline, exploring modular geometric construction.

name: OCR-X
classification_notes: Homage to OCR-A (1966), the first machine-readable typeface standardised for optical character recognition. Extended and reworked version with contemporary sensibility. Accompanied by a type specimen featuring an essay and ASCII comic.
summary: A reworking of the classic OCR-A typeface by Eurostandard and Maximage, published with a 48-page specimen featuring an essay and ASCII comic.
description: OCR-X is a collaborative typeface by Lausanne/Paris studio Eurostandard and Maximage, paying homage to OCR-A, the 1966 American Type Founders standard for machine-readable letterforms. The release includes a 48-page publication with a typeface essay and ASCII comic. It sits at the intersection of technical heritage and contemporary design research.
foundry_blurb: OCR-X is an homage to the classic OCR-A typeface from 1966, developed by Eurostandard and Maximage, accompanied by a 48-page specimen with an essay and ASCII comic.

name: Cornbread
classification_notes: Display typeface inspired by the handstyle of Darryl 'Cornbread' McCray, widely considered the first modern graffiti writer from Philadelphia. 5 styles.
summary: A display typeface drawn from the handstyle of Darryl 'Cornbread' McCray, widely credited as the pioneer of modern graffiti writing.
description: Cornbread is a display typeface based on the lettering of Darryl 'Cornbread' McCray, a Philadelphia graffiti artist regarded as one of the originators of modern graffiti writing. The release accompanies a publication, 'Cornbread the Legend', and spans 5 styles. It is a rare example of a type foundry giving formal release to vernacular letterforms from their originator.
foundry_blurb: Cornbread is a typeface inspired by the handstyle of Darryl 'Cornbread' McCray, one of the first modern graffiti writers, accompanied by a publication on his legacy.

name: Eurocat
classification_notes: Free display typeface designed by Giliane Cachin. 5 styles. Offered as a free download from Maxitype.
summary: A free experimental display typeface by Zürich-based designer Giliane Cachin, available in five styles.
description: Eurocat is a free display typeface designed by Giliane Cachin, a Zürich-based graphic designer who also created the Selecta type specimen for Maxitype. It is offered as a free download in five styles and reflects the foundry's commitment to supporting experimental typographic research beyond commercial releases.
foundry_blurb: Eurocat is a free typeface by Giliane Cachin, available to download in five styles from Maxitype.

name: Metaballs
classification_notes: Experimental display typeface by Maximage with 5 styles. The name suggests fluid, blob-like forms inspired by the metaball algorithm used in 3D graphics.
summary: An experimental display typeface by Maximage with liquid, blob-like forms drawn from metaball rendering concepts.
description: Metaballs is a display typeface by Maximage taking its name and visual inspiration from the metaball algorithm — a technique in computer graphics for rendering fluid, merging organic forms. The result is a typeface with letterforms that appear to bleed or merge at their junctions. It spans 5 styles and represents the more algorithmically playful end of Maxitype's catalogue.
foundry_blurb: Metaballs is an experimental typeface by Maximage exploring liquid, merging letterform shapes inspired by 3D metaball rendering.

name: Modelo
classification_notes: Free grotesque typeface by Maximage in 5 styles. Type specimen designed by Marietta Eugster. Offered as a free download.
summary: A free grotesque by Maximage available in five styles, with a type specimen by Marietta Eugster.
description: Modelo is a grotesque sans-serif by Maximage offered as a free download from Maxitype in 5 styles. Its type specimen was designed by Marietta Eugster, a Paris/Zurich-based art director. Like Eurocat, it represents Maxitype's practice of releasing select typefaces freely to support the design community.
foundry_blurb: Modelo is a free grotesque typeface by Maximage in five styles, with a type specimen designed by Marietta Eugster.

name: System
classification_notes: 8-style grotesque by Eurostandard. Clean, systematic design aligned with the Lausanne/Paris studio's functionalist approach. Name reflects an emphasis on systematic, rational design.
summary: A systematic grotesque sans-serif by Lausanne/Paris studio Eurostandard, spanning eight styles.
description: System is a grotesque typeface by Eurostandard, the Lausanne and Paris-based studio of Ali-Eddine Abdelkhalek, Pierrick Brégeon, Thomas Petit and Clément Rouzaud. It spans eight styles and embodies the studio's methodical, functionalist design approach. The typeface's name signals an interest in designed systems over expressive individualism.
foundry_blurb: System is a grotesque typeface by Eurostandard (Lausanne/Paris), available in eight styles with a systematic, functionalist approach to letter construction.
